case_vignette: A 34-year-old man with AIDS comes to the physician because of a 2-day history of decreasing vision and seeing black spots in his right eye. He has no pain and the left eye is asymptomatic. He was treated for fungal esophagitis 6 months ago with fluconazole. He was diagnosed with Kaposi's sarcoma 2 years ago. Current medications include efavirenz, tenofovir, emtricitabine, azithromycin, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole, multivitamins, and a nutritional supplement. He is 170 cm (5 ft 7 in) tall and weighs 45 kg (99 lbs);BMI is 15.6 kg/m2. His temperature is 37°C (98.6°F), pulse is 89/min, and blood pressure is 110/70 mm Hg. Examination shows cervical lymphadenopathy. There are multiple violaceous plaques seen over his trunk and extremities. Fundoscopic examination shows granular yellow-white opacities around the retinal vessels and multiple areas of dot-blot hemorrhages. His CD4+ T-lymphocyte count is 36/mm3.
question: Which  is the most likely diagnosis?

multiturn_conversation.content: You are an AI doctor specializing in Infectious Diseases. Arrive at a diagnosis of a patient's medical condition. Ask only one question at a time, and it should not be more than 1 line. Continue asking questions until you're 100% confident of the diagnosis. Do not ask the same question multiple times. Ask different questions to cover more information. The questions should cover age and sex of the patient, current symptoms, medical history of illness and medications, and relevant family history if necessary. Keep your questions short and brief to not confuse the patient.  After you're done asking questions, give the final diagnosis as a short response. Do not explain, only give the diagnosis name. You must state '**Final Diagnosis:**' at the beginning of your response, otherwise you will be penalized. You must give only 1 diagnosis otherwise you will be penalized. Note that you only have 40 turns, so ask only the necessary questions and make sure your conversation is brief. As the doctor, you may also get one message from an admin during your conversation with the tag [*INTERVENTION*] that gives you advice on how to approach the conversation.
Hi! What symptoms are you facing today?
I've had two days of worsening vision in my right eye and keep seeing black spots, but it doesn't hurt.
How old are you and what is your sex?
I am a 34-year-old man.
Do you see flashes of light or a curtain/veil coming over part of the vision?
No, I haven't noticed any flashes of light or any curtain-like covering over my vision.
Do you have diabetes, high blood pressure, or any blood-clotting disorders?
No, I don't have diabetes, high blood pressure, or any blood-clotting problems.
Any recent eye or head trauma?
No, I haven't had any recent injury to my eye or head.
Are the black spots mobile and move when you move your eyes?
I haven't noticed whether the black spots move when I move my eyes.
Do you have high nearsightedness (myopia) or have you ever had eye surgery (e.g., LASIK)?
No, I don't have bad nearsightedness and I've never had eye surgery.
Is your vision loss a general blurring or are the black spots blocking parts of your view?
The black spots are blocking parts of my right-side view.
Are you taking any medications, especially blood thinners (aspirin, warfarin, or DOACs)?
I'm taking HIV medicines (efavirenz, tenofovir, emtricitabine), azithromycin and trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole, plus vitamins and a nutritional supplement, but I'm not on any blood thinners.
Do you know your most recent CD4 count and HIV viral load?
My last CD4 count was 36 cells per mm3, and I don't know my HIV viral load.
Do the black spots move when you move your eyes?
I haven't noticed whether the black spots move when I move my eyes.
Is only your right eye affected or are both eyes involved?
Only my right eye is affected; my left eye has no symptoms.
Is your right eye red or do you have any discharge?
No, my right eye isn't red and I don't have any discharge.
Do you notice moving specks or floaters in the affected eye?
Yes, I see black moving spots (floaters) in my right eye.
**Final Diagnosis:** CMV retinitis
